blocker_notes.boxrec_anonymous_quota: BoxRec rate-limits anonymous requests to roughly 5-10 profile page views per IP per day. After those, the site 302-redirects to /en/login?error=limit. The public /en/suggest_search autocomplete endpoint has a separate, higher quota and continues to work — we use it for name->ID resolution. Node's native fetch is also blocked by Cloudflare (403) regardless of headers; only curl-class clients pass the TLS fingerprint check. Net result: for this pass we relied on Wikipedia infoboxes (which cite BoxRec IDs) as the authoritative source for pro-boxing records. Two BoxRec profile pages (Herring wrong-ID Cangiano + Malignaggi 52984) were fetched live before the quota exhausted.
mitigations: scripts/_boxrec-verified.json caches 10 Wikipedia-sourced records with BoxRec IDs so future reruns don't re-hit WebFetch
scrape-boxrec.mjs supports --use-verified flag for offline enrichment
Future auth'd run (with BoxRec paid sub + session cookie) could enrich the remaining ~50 priority fighters in one batch by passing Cookie header in fetchText()
